title: Roepera flexuosa
taxonomy: Roepera flexuosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is also known as the 'tangle vine' or 'tangle bush'. Roepera flexuosa is native to the western and southern parts of Australia. The plant is a climber or scrambler, with a tangled habit and small, yellow flowers. Roepera flexuosa is classified in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Sapindales.
uses: Roepera flexuosa is traditionally used in folk medicine, but its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
